speaker: LEONTES
text: O grave and good Paulina, the great comfort

speaker: LEONTES
text: That I have had of thee!

speaker: PAULINA
text: What, sovereign sir,

speaker: PAULINA
text: I did not well, I meant well. All my services

speaker: PAULINA
text: You have paid home. But that you have vouchsafed,

speaker: PAULINA
text: With your crowned brother and these your contracted

speaker: PAULINA
text: Heirs of your kingdoms, my poor house to visit,

speaker: PAULINA
text: It is a surplus of your grace which never

speaker: PAULINA
text: My life may last to answer.

speaker: LEONTES
text: O Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: We honor you with trouble. But we came

speaker: LEONTES
text: To see the statue of our queen. Your gallery

speaker: LEONTES
text: Have we passed through, not without much content

speaker: LEONTES
text: In many singularities; but we saw not

speaker: LEONTES
text: That which my daughter came to look upon,

speaker: LEONTES
text: The statue of her mother.

speaker: PAULINA
text: As she lived peerless,

speaker: PAULINA
text: So her dead likeness, I do well believe,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Excels whatever yet you looked upon

speaker: PAULINA
text: Or hand of man hath done. Therefore I keep it

speaker: PAULINA
text: Lonely, apart. But here it is. Prepare

speaker: PAULINA
text: To see the life as lively mocked as ever

speaker: PAULINA
text: Still sleep mocked death. Behold, and say ’tis well.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I like your silence. It the more shows off

speaker: PAULINA
text: Your wonder. But yet speak. First you, my liege.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Comes it not something near?

speaker: LEONTES
text: Her natural posture!—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Chide me, dear stone, that I may say indeed

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou art Hermione; or rather, thou art she

speaker: LEONTES
text: In thy not chiding, for she was as tender

speaker: LEONTES
text: As infancy and grace.—But yet, Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Hermione was not so much wrinkled, nothing

speaker: LEONTES
text: So agèd as this seems.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: O, not by much!

speaker: PAULINA
text: So much the more our carver’s excellence,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Which lets go by some sixteen years and makes her

speaker: PAULINA
text: As she lived now.

speaker: LEONTES
text: As now she might have done,

speaker: LEONTES
text: So much to my good comfort as it is

speaker: LEONTES
text: Now piercing to my soul. O, thus she stood,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Even with such life of majesty—warm life,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As now it coldly stands—when first I wooed her.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I am ashamed. Does not the stone rebuke me

speaker: LEONTES
text: For being more stone than it?—O royal piece,

speaker: LEONTES
text: There’s magic in thy majesty, which has

speaker: LEONTES
text: My evils conjured to remembrance and

speaker: LEONTES
text: From thy admiring daughter took the spirits,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Standing like stone with thee.

speaker: PERDITA
text: And give me leave,

speaker: PERDITA
text: And do not say ’tis superstition, that

speaker: PERDITA
text: I kneel, and then implore her blessing.

speaker: PERDITA
text: Lady,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Dear queen, that ended when I but began,

speaker: PERDITA
text: Give me that hand of yours to kiss.

speaker: PAULINA
text: O, patience!

speaker: PAULINA
text: The statue is but newly fixed; the color’s

speaker: PAULINA
text: Not dry.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: My lord, your sorrow was too sore laid on,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Which sixteen winters cannot blow away,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: So many summers dry. Scarce any joy

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Did ever so long live; no sorrow

speaker: CAMILLO
text: But killed itself much sooner.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Dear my brother,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Let him that was the cause of this have power

speaker: POLIXENES
text: To take off so much grief from you as he

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Will piece up in himself.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Indeed, my lord,

speaker: PAULINA
text: If I had thought the sight of my poor image

speaker: PAULINA
text: Would thus have wrought you—for the stone is mine—

speaker: PAULINA
text: I’d not have showed it.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Do not draw the curtain.

speaker: PAULINA
text: No longer shall you gaze on ’t, lest your fancy

speaker: PAULINA
text: May think anon it moves.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Let be, let be.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Would I were dead but that methinks already—

speaker: LEONTES
text: What was he that did make it?—See, my lord,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Would you not deem it breathed? And that those veins

speaker: LEONTES
text: Did verily bear blood?

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Masterly done.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: The very life seems warm upon her lip.

speaker: LEONTES
text: The fixture of her eye has motion in ’t,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As we are mocked with art.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I’ll draw the curtain.

speaker: PAULINA
text: My lord’s almost so far transported that

speaker: PAULINA
text: He’ll think anon it lives.

speaker: LEONTES
text: O sweet Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Make me to think so twenty years together!

speaker: LEONTES
text: No settled senses of the world can match

speaker: LEONTES
text: The pleasure of that madness. Let ’t alone.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I am sorry, sir, I have thus far stirred you, but

speaker: PAULINA
text: I could afflict you farther.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Do, Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: For this affliction has a taste as sweet

speaker: LEONTES
text: As any cordial comfort. Still methinks

speaker: LEONTES
text: There is an air comes from her. What fine chisel

speaker: LEONTES
text: Could ever yet cut breath? Let no man mock me,

speaker: LEONTES
text: For I will kiss her.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Good my lord, forbear.

speaker: PAULINA
text: The ruddiness upon her lip is wet.

speaker: PAULINA
text: You’ll mar it if you kiss it, stain your own

speaker: PAULINA
text: With oily painting. Shall I draw the curtain?

speaker: LEONTES
text: No, not these twenty years.

speaker: PERDITA
text: So long could I

speaker: PERDITA
text: Stand by, a looker-on.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Either forbear,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Quit presently the chapel, or resolve you

speaker: PAULINA
text: For more amazement. If you can behold it,

speaker: PAULINA
text: I’ll make the statue move indeed, descend

speaker: PAULINA
text: And take you by the hand. But then you’ll think—

speaker: PAULINA
text: Which I protest against—I am assisted

speaker: PAULINA
text: By wicked powers.

speaker: LEONTES
text: What you can make her do

speaker: LEONTES
text: I am content to look on; what to speak,

speaker: LEONTES
text: I am content to hear, for ’tis as easy

speaker: LEONTES
text: To make her speak as move.

speaker: PAULINA
text: It is required

speaker: PAULINA
text: You do awake your faith. Then all stand still—

speaker: PAULINA
text: Or those that think it is unlawful business

speaker: PAULINA
text: I am about, let them depart.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Proceed.

speaker: LEONTES
text: No foot shall stir.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Music, awake her! Strike!

speaker: PAULINA
text: ’Tis time. Descend. Be stone no more. Approach.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Strike all that look upon with marvel. Come,

speaker: PAULINA
text: I’ll fill your grave up. Stir, nay, come away.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Bequeath to death your numbness, for from him

speaker: PAULINA
text: Dear life redeems you.—You perceive she stirs.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Start not. Her actions shall be holy as

speaker: PAULINA
text: You hear my spell is lawful. Do not shun her

speaker: PAULINA
text: Until you see her die again, for then

speaker: PAULINA
text: You kill her double. Nay, present your hand.

speaker: PAULINA
text: When she was young, you wooed her; now in age

speaker: PAULINA
text: Is she become the suitor?

speaker: LEONTES
text: O, she’s warm!

speaker: LEONTES
text: If this be magic, let it be an art

speaker: LEONTES
text: Lawful as eating.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: She embraces him.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: She hangs about his neck.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: If she pertain to life, let her speak too.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Ay, and make it manifest where she has lived,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Or how stol’n from the dead.

speaker: PAULINA
text: That she is living,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Were it but told you, should be hooted at

speaker: PAULINA
text: Like an old tale, but it appears she lives,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Though yet she speak not. Mark a little while.

speaker: PAULINA
text:
To
                 
                Perdita
                .
                 
              Please you to interpose, fair madam.Kneel

speaker: PAULINA
text:
And pray your mother’s blessing.
                 
                To
                 
                Hermione
                .
              Turn, good lady.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Our Perdita is found.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: You gods, look down,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And from your sacred vials pour your graces

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Upon my daughter’s head! Tell me, mine own,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Where hast thou been preserved? Where lived? How found

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Thy father’s court? For thou shalt hear that I,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Knowing by Paulina that the oracle

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Gave hope thou wast in being, have preserved

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Myself to see the issue.

speaker: PAULINA
text: There’s time enough for that,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Lest they desire upon this push to trouble

speaker: PAULINA
text: Your joys with like relation. Go together,

speaker: PAULINA
text: You precious winners all. Your exultation

speaker: PAULINA
text: Partake to everyone. I, an old turtle,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Will wing me to some withered bough and there

speaker: PAULINA
text: My mate, that’s never to be found again,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Lament till I am lost.

speaker: LEONTES
text: O peace, Paulina.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou shouldst a husband take by my consent,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As I by thine a wife. This is a match,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And made between ’s by vows. Thou hast found mine—

speaker: LEONTES
text: But how is to be questioned, for I saw her,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As I thought, dead, and have in vain said many

speaker: LEONTES
text: A prayer upon her grave. I’ll not seek far—

speaker: LEONTES
text: For him, I partly know his mind—to find thee

speaker: LEONTES
text: An honorable husband.—Come, Camillo,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And take her by the hand, whose worth and honesty

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is richly noted and here justified

speaker: LEONTES
text: By us, a pair of kings. Let’s from this place.

speaker: LEONTES
text:
To
                 
                Hermione
                .
                 
              What, look upon my brother! Both your pardons

speaker: LEONTES
text: That e’er I put between your holy looks

speaker: LEONTES
text: My ill suspicion. This your son-in-law

speaker: LEONTES
text: And son unto the King, whom heavens directing,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is troth-plight to your daughter.—Good Paulina,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Lead us from hence, where we may leisurely

speaker: LEONTES
text: Each one demand and answer to his part

speaker: LEONTES
text: Performed in this wide gap of time since first

speaker: LEONTES
text: We were dissevered. Hastily lead away.
